note: Sprint 5.29 Trinity Scientific Intake Readers v0.1: extends scripts/trinity/scientific_prompt_intake.py (Sprint 5.20) with a pluggable per-extension reader registry. Default --allowed-ext now includes .tex, .csv and .pdf alongside the original .txt, .md, .json. Six new per-document fields land additive in the v0.1 schema: reader_kind enum {text, json, pdf, latex, csv, unsupported}, reader_status enum {ok, unsupported_extension, unsupported_missing_dependency, parse_error}, extracted_text_sha256 (64-hex of the deterministic extracted text), extracted_text_preview (bounded), structured_summary (reader-specific small dict), and per-document warnings. All six are in properties but NOT in required so the Sprint 5.20 strict-required-set schema test still passes; the Sprint 5.20 documents items shape test continues to enforce additionalProperties=false. combined_context_sha256 now mixes in extracted_text_sha256 and reader_status per document — a PDF that flips from unsupported_missing_dependency to ok because pypdf newly landed on the host changes the combined sha even though the raw bytes did not. Graceful PDF: _pdf_reader_module() tries pypdf then PyPDF2 via dynamic import; absence is recorded as unsupported_missing_dependency with a deterministic warning and an empty extracted text — no crash, no pip install, no subprocess shell-out to pdftotext. A static safety test enforces pypdf / PyPDF2 are NEVER imported at module top level — only inside the dynamic-import helper. Minimal LaTeX reader: prefers \begin{document}\end{document} body when present; strips comments, display math, inline math; rewrites \section / \textbf / \emph / \cite / etc. to keep the brace argument; drops bare \command tokens; regex only, no pandoc, no LaTeX runtime. CSV reader: stdlib csv only, row_count + column_count + optional header (heuristic: non-numeric first row) + preview_rows capped at 5, each cell capped at 256 chars. Unsupported extension branch: extensions in --allowed-ext but outside the reader registry (e.g. .yaml via --allowed-ext .yaml) are accepted, hashed, and recorded with reader_kind=unsupported / reader_status=unsupported_extension plus warnings; no crash. Extensions OUTSIDE --allowed-ext are still rejected outright with rc=2 — preserves the Sprint 5.20 gatekeeper contract verified by test_unknown_extension_rejected. Hard cap PDF_MAX_PAGES_TO_EXTRACT=200 prevents an adversarial PDF from blowing up the artifact; pages past the cap are skipped with a warning. Tests: 1191 passed, 38 skipped on the deployment host (1169 prior + 22 new = 16 functional + 6 safety; one functional test is pytest-skipped when pypdf/PyPDF2 not installed). One additional skip when pypdf / PyPDF2 not installed; the missing-dependency branch is tested deterministically on every host via monkeypatch.setattr(intake, '_pdf_reader_module', lambda: None). Manual demo with sample.md + sample.tex + sample.csv produces an intake artifact whose three documents carry reader_kind text + latex + csv, all reader_status=ok, deterministic extracted_text_sha256, and combined_context_sha256 honoring the new mix-in. Non-goals for v0.1 (documented): no OCR (PDF text-layer only), no pandoc, no XML / DOCX / EPUB / NetCDF / HDF5 / Parquet readers, no LLM call for extraction, no pip install of any dependency, no remote fetches. Schema id unchanged (trinity-scientific-prompt-intake/v0.1) — additive change. Pure scripts+schemas+docs+tests merge: zero src/, zero consensus changes, zero wallet/payment/broadcast changes.
